actus_reus: Physical act of taking property without consent

title: Theft in dwelling house
full_text: Whoever commits theft in any building, tent or vessel, which building, tent or vessel is used as a human dwelling, or used for the custody of property, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment for up to 7 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Theft must be committed
Theft must occur in a building, tent, or vessel
The building, tent, or vessel must be used as a human dwelling or for the custody of property
actus_reus: Committing theft in a dwelling house or a place used for custody of property

title: Theft by clerk or servant of property in possession of master
full_text: Whoever, being a clerk or servant, or being employed in the capacity of a clerk or servant, commits theft in respect of any property in the possession of his master or employer, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment for up to 7 years and fine
essential_ingredients: The person must be a clerk or servant
The person must commit theft
The property stolen must be in the possession of the master or employer
actus_reus: Committing theft of property in possession of master or employer

title: Punishment for extortion
full_text: Whoever commits extortion shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: The accused must commit extortion
The accused must intend to commit extortion
actus_reus: Committing extortion

title: Putting person in fear of injury in order to commit extortion
full_text: Whoever, in order to the committing of extortion, puts any person in fear, or attempts to put any person in fear, of any injury, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: The accused must put a person in fear or attempt to do so
The fear must be of any injury
The act must be done with the intention of committing extortion
actus_reus: Putting a person in fear or attempting to do so

title: Punishment for robbery
full_text: Whoever commits robbery shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine; and, if the robbery be committed on the highway between sunset and sunrise, the imprisonment may be extended to fourteen years.
punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for up to 10 years and fine, extendable to 14 years if committed on highway between sunset and sunrise
essential_ingredients: Committing robbery
Robbery committed on the highway between sunset and sunrise (for extended punishment)
actus_reus: The physical act of committing robbery

title: Attempt to commit robbery
full_text: Whoever attempts to commit robbery shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for up to 7 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Attempt to commit robbery
actus_reus: Attempt to commit robbery

title: Punishment for dacoity
full_text: Whoever commits dacoity shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for up to 10 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Committing dacoity
actus_reus: The physical act of committing dacoity
minimum_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for up to 10 years

title: Dacoity with murder
full_text: -If any one of five or more persons, who are conjointly committing dacoity, commits murder in so committing dacoity, every one of those persons shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life, or rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Death, or imprisonment for life, or rigorous imprisonment for up to ten years, and fine
essential_ingredients: Five or more persons conjointly committing dacoity
One of them commits murder during the dacoity
actus_reus: Conjointly committing dacoity and murder

title:
Attempt to commit robbery or dacoity when armed with deadly 
weapon
full_text: -If, at the time of attempting to commit robbery or dacoity, the offender is armed with any deadly weapon, the imprisonment with which such offender shall be punished shall not be less than seven years.
punishment: Imprisonment of not less than seven years
essential_ingredients: Attempt to commit robbery or dacoity
Armed with a deadly weapon at the time of attempt
actus_reus: Attempt to commit robbery or dacoity with a deadly weapon

title: Making preparation to commit dacoity
full_text: Whoever makes any preparation for committing dacoity, shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for up to 10 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Making preparation
For committing dacoity
actus_reus: Making preparation for dacoity

title: Punishment for belonging to gang of thieves
full_text: Whoever, at any time after the passing of this Act, shall belong to any wandering or other gang of persons associated for the purpose of habitually committing theft or robbery, and not being a gang of thugs or dacoits, shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for up to 7 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Belonging to a gang of persons
Gang associated for habitually committing theft or robbery
Not being a gang of thugs or dacoits
actus_reus: Belonging to a gang with the intention of habitually committing theft or robbery

title:
Dishonestly receiving property stolen in the commission of a 
dacoity
full_text: Whoever dishonestly receives or retains any stolen property, the possession whereof he knows or has reason to believe to have been transferred by the commission of dacoity, or dishonestly receives from a person, whom he knows or has reason to believe to belong or to have belonged to a gang of dacoits, property which he knows or has reason to believe to have been stolen, shall be punished with 4*[imprisonment for life], or with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for up to 10 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Dishonestly receiving or retaining stolen property
Knowledge or reason to believe the property was stolen in a dacoity
Knowledge or reason to believe the property was stolen by a gang of dacoits
actus_reus: Dishonestly receiving or retaining stolen property
minimum_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for up to 10 years

title: Habitually dealing in stolen property
full_text: Whoever habitually receives or deals in property which he knows or has reason to believe to be stolen property, shall be punished with 4*[imprisonment for life], or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Habitually receiving or dealing in property
Knowledge or reason to believe the property is stolen
actus_reus: Receiving or dealing in stolen property

title:
Assisting 
in concealment of stolen property
full_text: Whoever voluntarily assists in concealing or disposing of or making away with property which he knows or has reason to believe to be stolen property, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: The person must have voluntarily assisted
The assistance must be in concealing, disposing of, or making away with property
The person must know or have reason to believe the property is stolen
actus_reus: Voluntarily assisting in concealing or disposing of stolen property

title: Cheating
full_text: Whoever, by deceiving any person, fraudulently or dishonestly induces the person so deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to consent that any person shall retain any property, or intentionally induces the person so deceived to do or omit to do anything which he would not do or omit if he were not so deceived, and which act or omission causes or is likely to cause damage or harm to that person in body, mind, reputation or property, is said to "cheat". Explanation.-A dishonest concealment of facts is a deception within the meaning of this section. Illustrations (a) A, by falsely pretending to be in the Civil Service, intentionally deceives Z, and thus dishonestly induces Z to let him have on credit goods for which he does not mean to pay. A cheats.
punishment: Not explicitly mentioned in the section
essential_ingredients: Deception of any person
Fraudulent or dishonest inducement
Delivery or retention of property
Intentional act or omission causing damage or harm
actus_reus: Deception and inducement leading to delivery or retention of property
maximum_punishment: Not explicitly mentioned in the section
minimum_punishment: Not explicitly mentioned in the section

title: Punishment for cheating
full_text: Whoever cheats shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: The act of cheating
Intention to deceive
actus_reus: The act of cheating

title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
full_text: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security, or anything which is signed or sealed, and which is capable of being converted into a valuable security, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine. Of fraudulent deeds and dispositions of property
punishment: Imprisonment for up to 7 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Cheating
Dishonest induction
Delivery of property or alteration/destruction of valuable security
actus_reus: Delivering property or altering/destroying valuable security due to cheating

title: Dishonest or fraudulent removal or concealment of property
full_text: Whoever dishonestly or fraudulently conceals or removes any property of himself or any other person, or dishonestly or fraudulently assists in the concealment or removal thereof, or dishonestly releases any demand or claim to which he is entitled, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: Dishonest or fraudulent intent
Concealment or removal of property
Assistance in concealment or removal
Releasing a demand or claim dishonestly
actus_reus: Concealment or removal of property, or assistance in such acts

title: Mischief causing damage to the amount of fifty rupees
full_text: Whoever commits mischief and thereby causes loss or damage to the amount of fifty rupees or upwards, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: Committing mischief
Causing loss or damage
Loss or damage amounting to fifty rupees or upwards
actus_reus: Committing an act of mischief that results in damage

title:
Mischief by killing or maiming animal of the value of ten 
rupees
full_text: Whoever commits mischief by killing, poisoning, maiming or rendering useless any animals or animal of the value of the ten rupees or upwards, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: Killing, poisoning, maiming or rendering useless of an animal
The animal must be of the value of ten rupees or upwards
The act must be committed with the intention of causing mischief
actus_reus: Killing, poisoning, maiming or rendering useless of an animal

title:
Mischief by injury to public road, bridge, river or 
channel
full_text: Whoever commits mischief by doing any act which renders or which he knows to be likely to render any public road, bridge, navigable river or navigable channel, natural or artificial, impassable or less safe for travelling or conveying property, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: Committing mischief
Act renders public road, bridge, river, or channel impassable or less safe
Knowledge of the act's likely consequences
actus_reus: Doing an act that renders public infrastructure impassable or less safe

title:
Mischief committed after preparation made for causing death 
or hurt
full_text: Whoever commits mischief, having made preparation for causing to any person death, or hurt, or wrongful restraint, or fear of death, or hurt, or of wrongful restraint, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, and shall also be liable to fine. Of criminal trespass
punishment: Imprisonment of either description for up to 5 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Committing mischief
Having made preparation for causing death, hurt, or wrongful restraint
Intention to cause death, hurt, or wrongful restraint
actus_reus: Committing mischief with preparation for causing harm

title:
Lurking 
house-trespass
full_text: Whoever commits house-trespass having taken precautions to conceal such house-trespass from some person who has a right to exclude or eject the trespasser from the building, tent or vessel which is the subject of the trespass, is said to commit 'lurking house-trespass'.
punishment: Not specified in the given text
essential_ingredients: Committing house-trespass
Taking precautions to conceal the trespass
Concealment from a person with the right to exclude or eject the trespasser
actus_reus: Physical act of committing house-trespass with concealment
maximum_punishment: Not specified in the given text
minimum_punishment: Not specified in the given text

title: Lurking house-trespass by night
full_text: Whoever commits lurking house-trespass after sunset and before sunrise, is said to commit lurking house-trespass by night
punishment: Not specified in the section
essential_ingredients: Committing lurking house-trespass
The act is done after sunset and before sunrise
actus_reus: Physical act of lurking house-trespass

title: House-breaking
full_text: -A person is said to commit 'house-breaking' who commits house-trespass if he effects his entrance into the house or any part of it in any of the six ways hereinafter described; or if, being in the house or any part of it for the purpose of committing an offence, or having committed an offence therein, he quits the house or any part of it in any of it in such six ways, that is to say :­ First.-If he enters or quits through a passage made by himself, or by any abettor of the house-trespass, in order to the committing of the house-trespass.
punishment: Not mentioned in the text
essential_ingredients: Entering or quitting through a passage made by himself or an abettor
Entering or quitting for the purpose of committing an offence
Having committed an offence in the house
actus_reus: Entering or quitting a house through an unauthorized passage

title: Punishment for criminal trespass
full_text: Whoever commits criminal trespass shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months, or fine up to 500 rupees, or both
essential_ingredients: The person must have committed criminal trespass
actus_reus: The physical act of trespassing
maximum_punishment: 3 months imprisonment or 500 rupees fine or both

title:
House-trespass in order to commit offence punishable with 
death
full_text: Whoever commits house-trespass in order to the committing of any offence punishable with death, shall be punished with 1*[imprisonment for life], or with rigorous imprisonment for a term not exceeding ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment for life, or rigorous imprisonment for up to 10 years, and fine
essential_ingredients: Committing house-trespass
Intent to commit an offence punishable with death
actus_reus: Physical act of trespassing into a house

title:
House-trespass in order to commit offence punishable with 
imprisonment for life
full_text: Whoever commits house-trespass in order to the committing of any offence punishable with 1*[imprisonment for life], shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term not exceeding ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment for up to 10 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Committing house-trespass
Intent to commit an offence punishable with imprisonment for life
actus_reus: Physical act of trespassing into a house

title: Punishment for lurking house-trespass or house-breaking
full_text: Whoever commits lurking house-trespass or house-breaking, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Committing lurking house-trespass
Committing house-breaking
actus_reus: Physical act of trespassing or breaking into a house

title:
Punishment for lurking house-trespass or house-breaking by 
night
full_text: Whoever commits lurking house-trespass by night, or house­breaking by night, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment for up to 3 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Committing lurking house-trespass by night
Committing house-breaking by night
actus_reus: Physical act of trespassing or breaking into a house at night

title: Forgery
chapter_title: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
full_text: Whoever makes any false document or part of a document with intent to cause damage or injury, to the public or to any person, or to support any claim or title, or to cause any person to part with property, or to enter into any express or implied contract, or with intent to commit fraud or that fraud may be committed, commits forgery.
punishment: Not explicitly mentioned in the section
essential_ingredients: Making a false document or part of a document
Intent to cause damage or injury
Intent to support any claim or title
Intent to cause any person to part with property
Intent to enter into any express or implied contract
Intent to commit fraud or that fraud may be committed
actus_reus: Making a false document or part of a document
maximum_punishment: Up to 3 years or fine or both

title: Punishment for forgery
chapter_title: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
full_text: Whoever commits forgery shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: Committing forgery
actus_reus: The act of committing forgery

title: Forgery for purpose of cheating
chapter_title: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
full_text: Whoever commits forgery, intending that the document forged shall be used for the purpose of cheating, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment of either description for up to 7 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Committing forgery
Intent to use the forged document for cheating
actus_reus: Committing forgery with the intent to cheat

title: Forgery for purpose of harming reputation
chapter_title: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
full_text: Whoever commits forgery, intending that the document forged shall harm the reputation of any party, or knowing that it is likely to be used for that purpose, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Committing forgery
Intending to harm the reputation of any party
Knowing that the forged document is likely to be used for harming reputation
actus_reus: Committing forgery with the intention of harming reputation

title: Using as genuine a forged document or electronic record
chapter_title: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
full_text: Whoever fraudulently or dishonestly uses as genuine any document which he knows or has reason to believe to be a forged document, shall be punished in the same manner as if he had forged such document.
punishment: Punished in the same manner as if he had forged such document
essential_ingredients: The person must use a document as genuine
The person must know or have reason to believe the document is forged
The use must be fraudulent or dishonest
actus_reus: Using a forged document as genuine
maximum_punishment: Life imprisonment or imprisonment up to 10 years

title: Using a false property mark
chapter_title: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
full_text: Whoever marks any movable property or goods or any case, package or other receptacle containing movable property or goods, or uses any case, package or other receptacle having any mark thereon, in a manner reasonably calculated to cause it to be believed that the property or goods so marked, or any property or goods contained in any such receptacle so marked, belong to a person to whom they do not belong, is said to use a false property mark.
punishment: Not explicitly mentioned in the section
essential_ingredients: Marking movable property or goods with a false property mark
Using a case, package, or receptacle with a false property mark
Intent to cause it to be believed that the property belongs to someone else
actus_reus: Marking or using a false property mark on movable property or goods
maximum_punishment: Not explicitly mentioned in the section
minimum_punishment: Not explicitly mentioned in the section

title: Punishment for using a false property mark
chapter_title: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
full_text: Whoever uses any false property mark shall, unless he proves that he acted without intent to defraud, be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment for up to 1 year, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: Using a false property mark
Intent to defraud (unless proven otherwise)
actus_reus: Using a false property mark

title: Counterfeiting a property mark used by another
chapter_title: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
full_text: Whoever counterfeits any property mark used by any other person shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: The act of counterfeiting
The property mark must be used by another person
actus_reus: Counterfeiting a property mark used by another person

title:
Breach of contract to attend on and supply wants of helpless 
person
full_text: Whoever, being bound by a lawful contract to attend on or to supply the wants of any person who, by reason of youth, or of unsoundness of mind, or of a disease or bodily weakness, is helpless or incapable of providing for his own safety or of supplying his own wants, voluntarily omits so to do, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to two hundred rupees, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months, or fine up to 200 rupees, or both
essential_ingredients: The person must be bound by a lawful contract
The contract must be to attend on or supply the wants of a helpless person
The person must voluntarily omit to fulfill the contract
actus_reus: Voluntary omission to attend on or supply the wants of a helpless person
maximum_punishment: 3 months imprisonment, or 200 rupees fine, or both

title: Cohabitation caused by a man deceitfully inducing a belief of lawful marriage
full_text: -Every man who by deceit causes any woman who is not lawfully married to him to believe that she is lawfully married to him and to cohabit or have sexual intercourse with him in that belief, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment for up to 10 years and fine
essential_ingredients: The man must deceive the woman
The woman must believe she is lawfully married due to the deceit
The woman must cohabit or have sexual intercourse with the man based on this belief
actus_reus: Deceitful induction of belief of lawful marriage leading to cohabitation or sexual intercourse

title: Marriage ceremony fraudulently gone through without lawful marriage
full_text: Whoever, dishonestly or with a fraudulent intention, goes through the ceremony of being married, knowing that he is not thereby lawfully married, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Imprisonment for up to 7 years and fine
essential_ingredients: Dishonestly or with fraudulent intention
Going through a marriage ceremony
Knowing that the marriage is not lawful
actus_reus: Going through a marriage ceremony with knowledge of its illegality

title: Enticing or taking away or detaining with criminal intent a married woman
full_text: Whoever takes or entices away any woman who is and whom he knows or has reason to believe to be the wife of any other man, from that man, or from any person having the care of her on behalf of that man, with intent that she may have illicit intercourse with any person, or conceals or detains with that intent any such woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: The woman must be a married woman
The person must know or have reason to believe she is married
The person must take, entice, conceal, or detain the woman
The intent must be for illicit intercourse
actus_reus: Taking, enticing, concealing, or detaining a married woman

title: Printing or engraving matter known to be defamatory
full_text: Whoever prints or engraves any matter, knowing or having good reason to believe that such matter is defamatory of any person, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Simple imprisonment for up to 2 years, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: The person prints or engraves any matter
The matter is defamatory of any person
The person knows or has good reason to believe that the matter is defamatory
actus_reus: Printing or engraving defamatory matter

title:
Sale of printed or engraved substance containing defamatory 
matter
full_text: Whoever sells or offers for sale any printed or engraved substance containing defamatory matter, knowing that it contains such matter, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Simple imprisonment for up to 2 years, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: The substance must be printed or engraved
The substance must contain defamatory matter
The person must know that the substance contains defamatory matter
The person must sell or offer for sale the substance
actus_reus: Selling or offering for sale a printed or engraved substance containing defamatory matter

title: Punishment for criminal intimidation
full_text: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; If threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, etc. If threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, etc.--and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both; Imprisonment up to 7 years, or fine, or both if threat involves death, grievous hurt, etc.
essential_ingredients: The accused must have committed the offence of criminal intimidation
The threat must be to cause death, grievous hurt, destruction of property, or other specified offences
The threat must be made with the intention of causing alarm or annoyance
actus_reus: The act of making a threat with the intention of causing alarm or annoyance

title: Criminal intimidation by an anonymous communication
full_text: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation by an anonymous communication, or having taken precaution to conceal the name or abode of the person from whom the threat comes, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, in addition to the punishment provided for the offence by the last preceding section.
punishment: Imprisonment for up to 2 years, in addition to the punishment provided for the offence by the last preceding section
essential_ingredients: Committing the offence of criminal intimidation
Using an anonymous communication or concealing the name or abode of the person from whom the threat comes
actus_reus: Sending an anonymous threatening communication or concealing one's identity while making a threat

title: Word, gesture or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman
full_text: Whoever, intending to insult the modesty of any woman, utters any word, makes any sound or gesture, or exhibits any object, intending that such word or sound shall be heard, or that such gesture or object shall be seen, by such woman, or intrudes upon the privacy of such woman, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
punishment: Simple imprisonment for up to 1 year, or fine, or both
essential_ingredients: Intention to insult the modesty of a woman
Uttering a word, making a sound or gesture, or exhibiting an object
Intruding upon the privacy of the woman
actus_reus: Uttering a word, making a sound or gesture, or exhibiting an object with the intention to insult
